measurement.launch_bound_note: per_kernel_eager ~= per_launch_overhead => tiny kernels are launch-bound (CPU enqueue dominates GPU exec); this is the regime where un-captured launches tax the step.

model.verdict: CUDA-graph leg LARGELY CLOSED in the deployed stack: the drafter loopgraph + verify cudagraph already capture the static forward (captured launch count >> residual). Residual headroom is bounded by the data-dependent accept-walk + glue (~39 launches) and is second-order. Decode-path scatter avoidance (NOT a launch lever) is the real remaining denominator headroom.
model.armed_pending: PENDING land's real launch trace (lawine #147 --trace): the overlap-limited true tax (vs the N*plo upper bound here) and the exact residual un-captured set need the live step trace. ARMED to re-price when that trace lands.
model.caveat: N*plo is an UPPER bound; async launches overlap GPU exec so the true tax is the GPU-idle fraction (<= this). The deployed capture already removes the bulk; do not double-count this against the 1.2182 baseline, which is measured WITH capture on.

method: LOCAL A10G: per-launch overhead via eager N-kernel chain vs CUDA-graph replay; tree-step launch budget modeled from topology; residual bounded. No HF Job / no served-file change.
